snippet.title: I Ship a Production App With No Backend – This Is the Architecture | Mikkel Malmberg, Tether
snippet.description:
🔥 What if every new user made your app stronger – not your bill bigger? We show how they shipped Keet, a production messenger, with zero centralized backend – using cryptographic keys, signed messages, and deterministic state sync instead.

👉 This is local-first, P2P architecture that actually runs in production.

What's inside:
✅ Why your server is just a reducer — and how to remove it from the equation completely
✅ How to replace auth, storage, and state sync with cryptography and a deterministic log
✅ The Peer SDK Tether is building so any developer can ship P2P apps without thinking about the crypto layer

🎙 Mikkel Malmberg is a programmer and indie app builder – founder of Brainbow, a tiny product studio building platforms for creators. He's been shipping macOS apps, tools, and side projects since 2007, and recently stumbled into P2P.
Connect with Mikkel:
🔗 x.com/mikker
Explore what he ships:
🔗 mikkelmalmberg.com
Learn more about Tether:
🔗 tether.io

snippet.title: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini – parallel agents in one editor | Zed live demo | Gaauwe Rombouts
snippet.description:
Run Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini in parallel – without leaving your editor. 💫 Gaauwe Rombouts from Zed shows how agentic workflows actually work in practice, live on stage at React Summit ⚡

What's inside:
✅ How to run multiple AI agents simultaneously in isolated work trees — no context switching, no friction
✅ Zed's open agent architecture: use any agent (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) through one unified interface
✅ Live demo: real mistakes, real fixes, real parallel builds — what actually happens when you let agents loose

🎙 Gaauwe Rombouts is a Frontend Engineer at Zed, building the web experience around a code editor obsessed with speed and AI collaboration. As one of the few TypeScript developers in a Rust-first team, he bridges the gap between web and systems engineering – and knows how to keep three AI agents running live on stage without missing a beat.
Explore Gaauwe's work:
🔗  https://gaauwe.nl/
💻 Connect with Gaauwe:
🔗 https://github.com/gaauwe
Learn more about Zed:
🔗 https://zed.dev/

snippet.title: 3 Places Firebase Breaks Your Next.js UX (Auth, Firestore, Remote Config) | Rosário Fernandes
snippet.description:
🧩 Most Firebase flicker bugs aren't bugs. They're architectural mismatches between client SDKs and React Server Components. Here's how to fix all three – with FirebaseServerApp, onSnapshotResume, and Remote Config Server SDK.

What's inside:
⚡ The auth flicker: why your server renders a Sign In button even for logged-in users — and the fix
⚡ The Firestore flicker: offline cache + real-time updates = 2 extra renders. How to collapse them into zero
⚡ The Remote Config flicker: why feature flags flash on load and how server-side fetch eliminates it

🎙 Rosário P. Fernandes is a Developer Relations Engineer on the Firebase team at Google, helping developers build with Firebase and Generative AI across mobile and web. A passionate community builder, he's a regular presence at Android, Firebase, and Flutter developer events worldwide.
Connect with Rosário:
👉 https://twitter.com/thatfiredev
👉 https://linkedin.com/in/rosariopfernandes
👉 https://github.com/thatfiredev
👉 https://bsky.app/profile/thatfire.dev
Firebase:
👉 https://firebase.google.com/

📝 Talk: Taming the Flicker: Firebase Patterns for React Server Components | Rosario Fernandes

This talk was recorded at React Summit Amsterdam 2026: 
🔗 https://reactsummit.com/?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=RosarioFernandes

📌 Follow the link to watch the full version of all the conference talks, QnA’s with speakers and hands-on workshop recordings:  
🔗 https://gitnation.com/events/react-summit-2026?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=RosarioFernandes

🕐 Timestamps:
00:00 - "I'm Actually an Android Dev" — Rosário Takes the Stage
00:35 - Firebase Is a Client SDK. React Runs on the Server. Here's the Problem
01:18 - The Auth Flicker: Why Your App Shows a Login Button to Logged-In Users
01:57 - FirebaseServerApp: Fix Auth State Before the Page Even Loads
02:32 - Firestore Makes It Worse — Offline Cache + Real-Time = 3 Flickers Total
03:35 - toJSON() + onSnapshotResume: How to Serialize Your Way Out of the Flicker
04:39 - Remote Config on the Server: Feature Flags That Work Before JS Loads
05:16 - What's Next for Firebase on the Web (App Hosting, SQL Connect, Crashlytics)

snippet.title: One Rendering Layer for React, Angular & Vue. No Duplication - AG Grid Case Study | Stephen Cooper
snippet.description:
AG Grid supports React, Angular, Vue, and vanilla JS – from one codebase, without duplicating logic for each framework. Here's the architecture experiment behind it 🧪

What's inside:
⚡️ Why complex component logic can't live in the view layer – and where it should go instead
⚡️ How AG Grid stripped state, events, and tree structure out of framework components down to pure DOM primitives
⚡️ The abstraction layer that lets one component render natively in any framework – and why performance still holds

🎙 Stephen Cooper is Team Lead at AG Grid, 
where he's spent years deep in the codebase – optimizing grid performance, solving framework integrations, and profiling more render cycles than he'd care to admit. He brings practical, experience-based insights straight from production.
Connect with Stephen:
👉 https://twitter.com/scooperdev
👉 https://linkedin.com/in/scooper-dev
👉 https://github.com/StephenCooper
👉 https://scooper.dev/
Try AG Grid:
👉 https://www.ag-grid.com/
